description: A colouring substance that enters or bonds with a material is 'tinoa'. It is especially familiar in cloth and thread, though dyes also colour paper, leather, food, and other substances. 'kolai' (pigment) is a particulate colourant; registered 'thekiro tinoa' is ink defined by its writing use.
articulatory_notes: Dental 't' closes behind the upper teeth and releases into high-front 'i'. Dental nasal 'n' begins stressed rounded 'o', followed by a clean hiatus into final open 'a'.
usage_notes: The dyed material follows the ordinary clause pattern, and the dye may take 'roe' (with). Source, preparation, colourfastness, and safety can be named when the broad colouring material is not enough.

examples.translation: I change the cloth with dye.

pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Fibre, water, preparation, and time in the bath all change a dye's result. The same plant can leave another colour when one of those relations changes.

semantic_domains.aesthetic: a substance used to establish colour within a material
semantic_domains.creation: a colouring material applied to textiles, paper, food, and other substances
semantic_domains.physical: a colourant taken into the receiving material instead of lying there as loose particles
